skill.name: vr-ar
skill.description: Guidance for planning VR and AR experiences with comfort, platform fit, interaction design, spatial scale, and performance targets.
skill.summary: Plan VR and AR experiences with comfort-first design and clear performance targets.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The six static findings are false positives caused by normal VR/AR terminology and metadata. No code execution, network access, credential handling, prompt injection, or malicious behavior was found in the reviewed SKILL.md content.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged line 3, but the line is only the skill description about VR/AR development principles. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function, or security implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains descriptive metadata only. There is no crypto-related operation or weak algorithm reference.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged line 50, but the line describes seated and standing comfort modes. It does not reference cryptography or unsafe security behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is VR comfort settings. No cryptographic primitive, credential handling, or security-sensitive code appears on this line.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged line 93, but the line is a section heading for spatial design. It is unrelated to cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a Markdown heading. No executable content or security implementation exists at this location.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged line 29, but the line lists ARCore as an Android AR platform. This is product guidance, not host or network reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a platform selection table entry. There are no commands, file reads, system probes, or collection instructions.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged line 43, but the line advises avoiding camera shake for user comfort. It does not request system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is motion sickness prevention. The line contains no reconnaissance logic or operational instruction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged line 44, but the line recommends gradual movement to reduce discomfort. It is not related to scanning or environment discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is comfort guidance for movement design. No system commands, enumeration, or exfiltration paths are present.

content.user_title: Design Comfortable VR and AR Experiences
content.value_statement: VR and AR projects often fail when comfort, scale, and frame rate are treated late. This skill gives concise planning guidance for platform choice, interaction patterns, spatial design, and performance targets.
actual_capabilities: Compares common VR platforms, including Quest, PCVR, PSVR, and WebXR.
Compares common AR platforms, including ARKit, ARCore, WebXR, and HoloLens.
Lists comfort controls for locomotion, turning, seating, standing, and height calibration.
Provides target frame rates and resolution references for major headset classes.
Summarizes controller, hand tracking, and spatial interaction patterns.
Highlights anti-patterns that harm comfort, readability, scale, or performance.
limitations: It is a concise guidance reference, not a full engine implementation guide.
It does not test headset builds or measure real device performance.
It does not include platform store policies or certification checklists.
Performance targets may need updates for new headset hardware.

use_cases.title: Plan an Immersive Prototype
use_cases.description: Select a platform, movement style, and interaction model before building a VR or AR prototype.

use_cases.title: Review Comfort Risks
use_cases.description: Check a concept for camera motion, acceleration, frame rate, text size, and reach problems.

use_cases.title: Set Technical Targets
use_cases.description: Define frame budgets, resolution expectations, and spatial scale requirements for an immersive app.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Platform
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the vr-ar skill to compare VR and AR platforms for my project. Recommend the best fit and explain the tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Player Comfort
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the vr-ar skill to review this movement design for motion sickness risks. Suggest comfort settings and safer alternatives.

prompt_templates.title: Define Interaction Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the vr-ar skill to design controller and hand tracking interactions for this immersive workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an Immersive Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the vr-ar skill to audit this VR or AR design for platform fit, comfort, performance, spatial scale, and anti-patterns.

output_examples.input: Review a VR museum tour concept with smooth walking and small wall labels.
output_examples.output: The review would recommend teleport or snap-turn options, larger readable labels, stable camera control, and a 90 FPS target.

output_examples.input: Plan an AR maintenance guide for Android tablets and enterprise headsets.
output_examples.output: The output would compare ARCore and HoloLens, then outline depth cues, readable overlays, and physical reach constraints.

output_examples.input: Check a Quest prototype with hand tracking and physics-based object manipulation.
output_examples.output: The response would flag precision limits for hand tracking and recommend clear grab states, realistic scale, and frame-time discipline.

best_practices: Start with comfort, frame rate, and movement rules before expanding content.
Use real-world scale and test object reach with representative users.
Give users control over turning, locomotion, height, and seated or standing modes.
anti_patterns: Do not move the camera independently of player intent.
Do not rely on small UI text in headset or mobile AR views.
Do not treat low frame rate as acceptable in VR experiences.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps plan VR and AR experiences around comfort, interaction design, platform selection, spatial scale, and performance targets.

faq.question: Does it write engine-specific code?
faq.answer: No. It provides design and planning guidance, not Unity, Unreal, or WebXR implementation code.

faq.question: Can it replace device testing?
faq.answer: No. You still need headset or device testing to validate comfort, readability, tracking, and frame rate.

faq.question: Which platforms does it mention?
faq.answer: It covers Quest, PCVR, PSVR, WebXR, ARKit, ARCore, and HoloLens at a high level.

faq.question: Is it useful for accessibility reviews?
faq.answer: Yes, it can surface comfort and readability issues, but it is not a complete accessibility standard.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
